verdict.answer_first: B states the core answer immediately: "This has a SQL injection vulnerability"; A also starts directly with "textbook SQL injection vulnerability" but takes longer overall.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder: "don't merge as-is" and "parameterized queries are the only answer you should ship"; B is clear but softer with "cleanup" notes.
verdict.fabrication: A overreaches with "closes the hole completely" and "structurally impossible"; B is more careful with "on a driver that allows stacked statements".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the fix first and keeps extras clearly secondary: "The parameterization is the one that matters".
